narrative:
The Zurich Zebras enter today’s slate with significant firepower at the top of the lineup, led by Trea Turner (82.90) and Christian Yelich (81.67). Both stars are primed for high-output performances, capitalizing on ideal platoon splits and prime batting order spots. Junior Caminero (77.09) and Pete Crow-Armstrong (75.71) also project as high-efficiency starters; Caminero benefits from a strong cleanup spot, while Crow-Armstrong’s matchup against Johnson, combined with his favorable wind conditions, makes him a priority play for stolen base and run-scoring potential.

Our mid-tier decisions require cautious management today, particularly regarding the platoon-constrained Yordan Alvarez and Kyle Schwarber. While both carry the risk of L/L disadvantages, their elite xwOBA metrics justify keeping them in the lineup for their inherent power ceiling. We are keeping a close eye on the bottom of the order where Jerar Encarnacion and Dillon Dingler face “Lineup Pending” designations; if they drop out of the confirmed batting order, we may need to rotate in bench depth to maintain 5x5 Roto volume. Additionally, Josh Smith and Christian Yelich carry the benefit of positive historical BvP data, providing a secondary edge in their respective matchups against Bassitt and Martinez.
